senses.definition: Relating to or resembling crabs of the infraorder Brachyura (short-tailed crabs).
examples: The fossil shows brachyuranic features typical of true crabs.
Brachyuranic characteristics helped scientists classify the specimen.

senses.definition: Crabs of the infraorder Brachyura; true crabs with broad, short bodies.
examples: Several brachyurans were observed scuttling along the shore.
The museum exhibit featured brachyurans from tropical reefs.

senses.definition: A misspelling or variant of 'brachyura' or related taxonomic term; not a standard English word.
examples: The field notes used brachyure, though the correct term is brachyura.
Glossaries should correct brachyure to the proper taxonomic name.

senses.definition: Relating to or resembling crabs of the infraorder Brachyura; short-tailed.
examples: The brachyurous legs adapted for quick sideways movement were observed.
Brachyurous features distinguish this crab from others.

senses.definition: A Latin-derived specific epithet used in scientific names for some crab species; not common in everyday English outside taxonomy.
examples: The species was named with the epithet brachyurus in the genus X.
Taxonomic papers often cite brachyurus Latin names.

senses.definition: Providing or showing firm, supportive strength; giving courage or confidence.
examples: The bracing sea air woke me up.
Her bracing words gave the team new energy.

senses.definition: To prepare a structure to resist pressure or to support it; to fasten or secure.
examples: They braced the door against the storm.
Bracing the beams helped stabilize the house.

senses.definition: In a way that is strengthening, invigorating, or encouraging.
examples: The cold wind blew bracingly across the harbor.
She spoke bracingly about the challenges ahead.

senses.definition: The quality of being bracing; the tendency to invigorate or reinforce.
examples: The bracingness of the sea air revived their spirits.
There was a bracingness to his critique that sharpened the discussion.

senses.definition: Elements that brace or support structures, such as beams or supports.
examples: The scaffolding used metal bracings for stability.
Bracings were added to the frame to prevent swaying.

senses.definition: A small Italian ragout or rolled slice of meat, often stuffed; used in cooking.
examples: The chef prepared braciola with garlic and herbs.
Braciola is often served with tomato sauce and sides.

senses.definition: Plural of braciola; several pieces of stuffed meat prepared in the Italian style.
examples: The menu featured two braciolas: beef and pork varieties.
They prepared braciolas for the family gathering.

senses.definition: Plural of braciola; a rolled and stuffed piece of meat in Italian cuisine.
examples: Braciole simmer in sauce to become tender.
Classic braciole are thinly sliced beef wrapped around breadcrumbs.

senses.definition: Variant plural form of braciole in English usage; refers to multiple stuffed meat pieces.
examples: The platter included several bracioles glazed with wine sauce.

senses.definition: Plural form used in English for stuffed rolled meats in Italian cuisine; synonymous with braciola.
examples: The braciole were served over pasta.
Recipes vary in how they stuff and flavor the braciole.

senses.definition: Alternative plural form used in English for braciole/braciola dishes.
examples: The bracioles were arranged neatly on the platter.

senses.definition: A type of salt or salt source; a mineral deposit often used in rock salt forms; also used as a verb meaning to coast with rough or dull sound; also a surname and place name.
examples: The rock contained brack from ancient seas.
The fisherman bracked the boat along the shore.

senses.definition: A mineral named brackebuschite; a rare phosphate mineral.
examples: They identified brackebuschite crystals in the rock sample.

senses.definition: Having a bracket or braces; pinched; not standard usage; possibly misspelling of 'bracked' meaning 'bracketed'.
examples: The text was bracked to indicate optional sections.

senses.definition: A large fern that grows in forests and hills; also the plant it comes from.
examples: The hikers walked through the bracken.
Bracken covers many hillside slopes.

senses.definition: Having been bracken-covered or overgrown with bracken; choked by ferns.
examples: The old trail was brackened and difficult to follow.

senses.definition: Plural of bracken; several large ferns.
examples: The hillside was covered with brackens.

senses.definition: A person who braids or braces; or a device that braces; also a surname; context needed.
examples: The bracker secured the frame.
Mr. Bracker spoke at the conference.

senses.definition: A support attached to a wall or structure; a curved or angled piece used to connect or hold things; a set of items enclosed by a bracket; in mathematics, a pair of symbols [ ] used to group expressions.
examples: The shelf is held up by a metal bracket.
Please put the note in brackets.

senses.definition: To place text or items inside brackets; to categorize or group under a heading.
examples: Bracket the cited passages with brackets.
The sections are bracketed for clarity.

senses.definition: Enclosed in brackets; noted or marked with brackets.
examples: The bracketed notes provide additional information.
Bracketed terms are not part of the main text.

senses.definition: The act of placing items inside brackets; the process of grouping or classifying.
examples: The bracketing of citations helps readers find sources.
Bracketing is used to indicate optional sections.

senses.definition: Plural form of bracketings; instances of bracketed groupings or the act of bracketing.
examples: Different bracketings were used in the draft.

senses.definition: Plural of bracket; devices or marks used to enclose or group; also a set of items in a category; in sports, a tournament draw.
examples: The shelves are held by metal brackets.
The tournament brackets showed the matchups.

senses.definition: To place something inside brackets; to separate into categories or sections.
examples: Bracket the uncertain items for later review.
